title: Framing Effect

content.statement: The same fact produces different decisions depending on how it is framed.
content.question: How would the decision change if I framed it the opposite way, and which frame is the truer one?
content.equation: Frame Gap = Response Rate A − Response Rate B
content.mental_model: Two windows on the same room, different views, different feelings.
content.business_lens: A/B test gain vs loss framing, percentage vs absolute framing, and time-frame variants in marketing.
content.life_lens: When you face a hard decision, reframe it deliberately to reveal your real preference.
sop: Use the more effective frame ethically
content.explainer: In a classic study, doctors recommended a treatment more often when told it had "90% survival" than "10% mortality", same fact, different decisions. Frames matter enormously; honest framing is a competitive advantage.
questions: How would the decision change if I framed it the opposite way, and which frame is the truer one?
Pick the last number you led with in a sales conversation, price, timeline, percentage. What's the honest alternate frame, and why didn't you use it?
If a competitor described your product using your own facts but the opposite frame, would it still sound appealing?
content.equation_example: Frame A (loss-framed): 18% conversion. Frame B (gain-framed): 11% conversion. Frame Gap = 18 − 11 = 7 percentage points. A bigger gap means frame choice is doing heavy lifting in your funnel — pick the winner and standardize it; a gap near 0 means your audience responds to substance more than wording, so frame less and clarify more.
content.closing_statement: The same facts can lead to opposite decisions.

caption:
Principle Nº 296: Framing Effect

The same fact produces different decisions depending on how it is framed. In a classic study, doctors recommended a treatment more often when told it had "90% survival" than "10% mortality", same fact, different decisions.

Ask yourself: How would the decision change if I framed it the opposite way, and which frame is the truer one?
